footer.gpcStatement: We honor the Global Privacy Control (GPC) signal.
footer.sanctionStatement: Restricted in sanctioned jurisdictions per US/EU/UK law.

cookie.title: Cookies and your data
cookie.body: We use cookies for essential functionality. Optional analytics, advertising, and personalization cookies are off by default. Choose what you allow.
cookie.essentialDescription: Required for the site to work. Always on.
cookie.analyticsDescription: Helps us understand which pages are useful.

ageGate.title: Confirm you're 18 or older

ageGate.underageError: You must be at least 18 years old to use RuleSell.
ageGate.sanctionedError: RuleSell is currently unavailable in your jurisdiction.

legal.disclaimer: This is a placeholder template prepared for product review. Final legal text must be reviewed by qualified counsel before public launch. It does not constitute legal advice.

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.intro: These Terms of Service ("Terms") govern your access to and use of RuleSell, a marketplace for AI development assets. By creating an account or using the service you agree to be bound by these Terms.

acceptance.title: 1. Acceptance of these Terms
acceptance.body: By accessing RuleSell you confirm that you have read, understood, and agreed to these Terms. If you do not agree, do not use the service. We may update these Terms from time to time; material changes will be announced at least 30 days before they take effect (DSA Art. 14).

eligibility.title: 2. Eligibility and the 18+ requirement
eligibility.body: RuleSell is open to natural persons aged 18 or older and to legal entities lawfully able to enter contracts. We do not knowingly process the personal data of children under 18. By signing up you confirm your date of birth and country of residence. Misrepresentation of age is a material breach (16 CFR 312, COPPA update effective 22 April 2026).

geographicRestrictions.title: 3. Geographic restrictions
geographicRestrictions.body: RuleSell is unavailable to residents of comprehensively sanctioned jurisdictions, including Cuba, Iran, North Korea, Syria, and the Crimea, Donetsk, and Luhansk regions of Ukraine. Additional sector-specific restrictions may apply to Belarus and the Russian Federation. We use both signup-time geo-blocking and transaction-time screening through our payment processors (US OFAC, EU Council Regulations, UK OFSI).

account.title: 4. Account registration and security
account.body: You must provide accurate information and keep your credentials confidential. You are responsible for activity carried out under your account. Notify us immediately of any unauthorised access. We may suspend or terminate accounts that compromise the security of the service or its users.

content.title: 5. Your content and our license
content.body: You retain all rights in the rulesets, prompts, agents, datasets, and other items you publish. By publishing on RuleSell you grant us a worldwide, non-exclusive, royalty-free licence to host, distribute, display, and transmit your content for the purpose of operating the marketplace. Paid items are also covered by the separate Creator Agreement.

prohibited.title: 6. Prohibited content and conduct
prohibited.body: You must not publish or share content that is illegal, infringes intellectual property, contains malware, harvests credentials, depicts the sexual exploitation of minors, incites violence or hatred, or otherwise breaches our Acceptable Use Policy. Violations may result in removal, account suspension, and referral to authorities (DSA Art. 16, Acceptable Use Policy).

moderation.title: 7. How we moderate content
moderation.body: We operate a notice-and-action system. Anyone may report content via the Report button on every listing, review, and profile. We confirm receipt and act without undue delay. Where we remove or demote content, the affected user receives a Statement of Reasons explaining the legal basis, the facts relied on, and the available redress (DSA Art. 16-17).

thirdParty.title: 8. Third-party content disclaimer
thirdParty.body: RuleSell hosts third-party content created by our users. We do not endorse and are not responsible for the accuracy, security, legality, or fitness for purpose of any third-party item. To the extent permitted by law, we act as an intermediary service provider and rely on the conditional liability exemptions of EU and US law (DSA Art. 6-8; 47 U.S.C. § 230(c)(1)).

termination.title: 9. Termination
termination.body: You may close your account at any time from /dashboard/settings/privacy. We may suspend or terminate accounts that breach these Terms, the Acceptable Use Policy, or applicable law. On termination your published items are unpublished, your reviews are kept but anonymised, and pending payouts are processed in line with the Creator Agreement.

disclaimers.title: 10. Disclaimers and limitation of liability
disclaimers.body: RuleSell is provided "as is" without warranties of any kind. To the maximum extent permitted by law, our aggregate liability for any claim arising out of or related to the service is limited to the greater of (a) the fees you paid us in the twelve months preceding the claim or (b) one hundred euros. Nothing in these Terms limits liability that cannot lawfully be limited.

law.title: 11. Governing law and disputes
law.body: These Terms are governed by the laws of the jurisdiction where the contracting RuleSell entity is established. EU consumers retain the protections of the mandatory laws of their country of residence. Where required, disputes may be referred to the European Commission's online dispute resolution platform.

changes.title: 12. Changes to these Terms
changes.body: We will notify registered users of material changes by email or in-product banner at least 30 days before the change takes effect. Continued use after the effective date constitutes acceptance. Earlier versions are kept in our changelog.

contact.title: 13. Contact
contact.body: Questions about these Terms can be sent to legal@rulesell.example. Our EU legal representative will be named here once appointed (DSA Art. 13).

terms.citations: DSA Art. 13, 14, 16, 17 (Regulation 2022/2065); GDPR Art. 6 (Regulation 2016/679); 47 U.S.C. § 230(c)(1); 16 CFR Part 312 (COPPA, 22 April 2026 update); US OFAC SDN List.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: This Privacy Policy explains what personal data RuleSell collects, why we collect it, who we share it with, and the rights you have over it. It covers all RuleSell websites, applications, and APIs.

scope.title: Who this applies to
scope.body: This policy applies to anyone who visits RuleSell or uses an account, regardless of where they live. The GDPR (Regulation 2016/679, Art. 3) applies to all processing of personal data of users in the European Union or European Economic Area. The CCPA and CPRA (Cal. Civ. Code § 1798.100 et seq.) apply to residents of California. We voluntarily extend these protections to all users.

controller.title: Who is the data controller
controller.body: RuleSell is the controller for the personal data we process for our own purposes. Our EU legal representative will be appointed before public launch under DSA Art. 13 and named here. The compliance contact is privacy@rulesell.example.

categories.title: What we collect
categories.body: We collect: account data (name, username, email, password hash); profile data (bio, avatar, preferred environments); content data (rulesets, reviews, comments); commerce data (purchase records, payout details handled by Stripe); technical data (IP address, device, browser, language, referer); and consent data (cookie choices, GPC signal, age and country self-declaration). We do not deliberately collect special categories of personal data.

purposes.title: Why we use it and on what legal basis
purposes.body: Account creation, marketplace operation, payments, fraud prevention, security, and legal compliance are processed on the basis of contract performance (GDPR Art. 6(1)(b)) or legal obligation (Art. 6(1)(c)). Optional analytics, advertising measurement, and personalisation are processed on the basis of your consent (Art. 6(1)(a)). Legitimate interest (Art. 6(1)(f)) is used for service improvement, debugging, and security telemetry.

retention.title: How long we keep it
retention.body: Account data is kept for the lifetime of your account. After deletion, anonymised review data may be retained. Tax and accounting records are kept for the period required by applicable law (typically 7-10 years). Security logs are kept for 90 days. Consent records are kept for 24 months.

sharing.title: Who we share it with
sharing.body: We share data only with vetted processors under signed Data Processing Agreements (GDPR Art. 28): hosting (Vercel), database (Neon Postgres), payments (Stripe Connect), email (Resend), error monitoring (Sentry). A current vendor list is maintained internally and available on request. We do not sell or share personal data for cross-context behavioural advertising; this is reflected in the "Do Not Sell or Share" footer link (CCPA § 1798.120, CPRA add).

transfers.title: International data transfers
transfers.body: Some processors are established in the United States. Transfers from the EEA to the US rely on the EU-US Data Privacy Framework where the processor is certified, or on the European Commission's Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs) supplemented by a transfer impact assessment (post-Schrems II).

rights.title: Your rights
rights.body: You can exercise the following rights at any time from /dashboard/settings/privacy: access (Art. 15), rectification (Art. 16), erasure (Art. 17), restriction (Art. 18), portability (Art. 20), and objection (Art. 21). California residents have parallel rights to know, delete, correct, and limit the use of their personal information (CCPA § 1798.100). We respond within 30 days (GDPR Art. 12(3)) or 45 days (CCPA), and free of charge.

gpc.title: Honoring the Global Privacy Control
gpc.body: RuleSell honors the Sec-GPC: 1 browser signal as a valid opt-out of sale and sharing of personal information under CCPA/CPRA. When detected, we set the gpc_honored cookie and apply the opt-out automatically.

children.title: Children
children.body: RuleSell is not directed to anyone under 18. We do not knowingly collect personal data from children under 18. The 18+ age gate at signup is an active block, and we will delete any account that turns out to belong to a minor (COPPA, 16 CFR Part 312, updated 22 April 2026 to cover biometric data).

security.title: Security and breach notification
security.body: We use industry-standard technical and organisational measures to protect personal data: TLS in transit, AES-256 at rest, role-based access control, audited dependency baseline, and two-factor authentication for staff. If we become aware of a personal data breach that poses a risk to rights and freedoms, we will notify the supervisory authority within 72 hours (GDPR Art. 33) and affected users without undue delay where required by Art. 34.

automated.title: Automated decisions
automated.body: We do not use solely automated decisions that produce legal or similarly significant effects on you. Quality scores and ranking signals influence display order, not access. A detailed Automated Decision-Making assessment (CPRA § 1798.121) will be published before we cross 1,000 monthly active users.

changes.title: Changes to this policy
changes.body: Material changes are announced at least 30 days in advance. The current version is always reachable from this URL with the "Last updated" date in the header.

contact.title: How to contact us
contact.body: Privacy questions: privacy@rulesell.example. EU representative: to be appointed before public launch (DSA Art. 13). Data Protection Officer: not appointed at launch — RuleSell does not meet the GDPR Art. 37 mandatory thresholds at this scale; an external DPO will be engaged before crossing 1,000 monthly active users.

cookies.title: Cookie Policy
cookies.intro: RuleSell uses cookies and similar technologies to operate the marketplace, remember your preferences, and (with your consent) measure how the service is used. This policy explains what each category does and how you can control them.

what.title: What is a cookie
what.body: A cookie is a small text file stored on your device by your browser when you visit a website. It can hold a session token, a preference, or an identifier. Similar technologies include localStorage, sessionStorage, and small first-party requests; this policy covers all of them collectively as "cookies".

consent.title: How we ask for consent
consent.body: On your first visit you see a cookie banner with three equally weighted choices: Accept all, Reject all, and Customize. You can change your choice at any time from /dashboard/settings/privacy. We treat the absence of consent as a refusal. Consent records are stored locally as a tamper-evident audit trail (DSA Art. 25, GDPR Art. 5(3)).

categories.title: Cookie categories
categories.body: Strictly Necessary cookies are required for the site to function and cannot be turned off. They include session cookies, CSRF tokens, locale preference, and the consent record itself. Functional cookies remember non-essential preferences such as theme. Analytics cookies help us understand which pages are useful in aggregate. Advertising cookies measure paid placements; we do not use them for cross-context behavioural advertising at launch.

list.title: Cookies we set
list.body: rulesell:consent — purpose: store consent choices; duration: 12 months; provider: RuleSell; category: Strictly Necessary. NEXT_LOCALE — purpose: language preference; duration: 12 months; provider: RuleSell; category: Strictly Necessary. gpc_honored — purpose: record that the Sec-GPC signal was honored; duration: session; provider: RuleSell; category: Strictly Necessary. dev:persona — purpose: development persona switcher (non-production only); duration: 30 days; provider: RuleSell; category: Strictly Necessary.

gpc.title: Global Privacy Control
gpc.body: If your browser sends the Sec-GPC: 1 header, RuleSell treats it as a valid opt-out of the sale and sharing of personal information under CCPA/CPRA. The opt-out applies regardless of your cookie banner choices and persists across sessions.

control.title: How to control cookies
control.body: You can change your consent at any time from /dashboard/settings/privacy. You can also clear or block cookies through your browser settings. Blocking strictly necessary cookies will break parts of RuleSell, including signing in.

cookies.citations: GDPR Art. 5(3), 7 (Regulation 2016/679); ePrivacy Directive 2002/58/EC; DSA Art. 25 (no dark patterns); CCPA § 1798.120, CPRA § 1798.135.

acceptableUse.title: Acceptable Use Policy
acceptableUse.intro: This policy lists the categories of content and behaviour that are not allowed on RuleSell. It applies to every account and supplements our Terms of Service. We update it regularly and keep an audit trail of changes.

prohibited.title: Prohibited content
prohibited.body: You may not publish, link to, or distribute through RuleSell: malware, ransomware, droppers, keyloggers, or remote-access trojans; content that infringes third-party copyright, trademarks, or patents; sexual content involving minors (CSAM); content that incites violence, terrorism, or genocide; content that targets people based on protected characteristics with hatred or harassment; spam, scraped data, or content that exists primarily to manipulate search rankings; live credentials, API keys, tokens, or cryptographic secrets; instructions for the construction of weapons capable of mass harm; content that violates the law of the user's jurisdiction or our own.

behaviour.title: Prohibited behaviour
behaviour.body: You may not: probe, scan, or test the vulnerability of RuleSell or any connected system without prior written permission; use automated systems to exceed published rate limits; impersonate another person, team, or organisation; sell, transfer, or rent your account; circumvent geographic, age, or content restrictions; misrepresent the licence, authorship, or quality of an item; submit fake reviews or coordinate review brigading.

consequences.title: Consequences
consequences.body: Where we identify a breach we may, depending on severity, issue a warning, demote a listing, unpublish an item, suspend or terminate an account, withhold pending payouts, refer the matter to law enforcement, and/or seek injunctive relief. Where we act we provide a Statement of Reasons (DSA Art. 17) explaining the legal basis, the facts relied on, and the available appeal route.

report.title: How to report a breach
report.body: Use the Report button on the offending listing, review, or profile, or visit /report. We confirm receipt and act without undue delay (DSA Art. 16). Reporters acting in good faith are protected from retaliation.

acceptableUse.citations: DSA Art. 14, 16, 17 (Regulation 2022/2065); 18 U.S.C. § 2258A (CSAM reporting); applicable export-control and dual-use regulations.

dmca.title: DMCA & Intellectual Property Policy
dmca.intro: RuleSell respects intellectual property and complies with the notice-and-takedown procedure of the United States Digital Millennium Copyright Act (DMCA). This page explains how to file a copyright notice, how counter-notices work, and our repeat-infringer policy.

agent.title: Designated DMCA agent
agent.body: Our designated agent for receiving notifications of claimed infringement is registered with the United States Copyright Office under § 512(c)(2). Until appointment is finalised, notices may be sent to dmca@rulesell.example. We will publish the registered agent's name, address, and registration number on this page before public launch.

notice.title: How to file a takedown notice
notice.body: A valid DMCA notice must include: (1) a physical or electronic signature of the rights holder or authorised agent; (2) identification of the copyrighted work claimed to be infringed; (3) the URL or sufficient detail to locate the allegedly infringing material on RuleSell; (4) your contact information; (5) a statement that you have a good-faith belief the use is not authorised; (6) a statement under penalty of perjury that the information is accurate and that you are authorised to act on behalf of the rights holder.

process.title: What we do with a notice
process.body: When we receive a complete notice we promptly disable access to the material or item in question and notify the user who posted it. We forward the notice to the user with a Statement of Reasons (DSA Art. 17). The original poster may submit a counter-notice.

counterNotice.title: Counter-notice procedure
counterNotice.body: A valid counter-notice must include: (1) your physical or electronic signature; (2) identification of the material removed; (3) a statement under penalty of perjury that you have a good-faith belief the material was removed by mistake or misidentification; (4) your name, address, and telephone number; (5) consent to the jurisdiction of the federal court for the district where you live (or for non-US users, the district where RuleSell may be found). On receipt of a valid counter-notice we will restore the material within 10-14 business days unless the original complainant files a court action.

repeat.title: Repeat-infringer policy
repeat.body: We terminate, in appropriate circumstances, the accounts of users who are repeat infringers, in accordance with § 512(i)(1)(A). "Repeat" is assessed against the totality of the conduct, not a fixed strike count, and gives weight to bad-faith notices on both sides.

license.title: License compliance for sellers
license.body: Every published item must declare a valid SPDX license identifier. Items with non-commercial licenses (e.g. CC BY-NC, CC BY-NC-SA) cannot be sold paid. The publishing wizard enforces this and the License Warning component shows incompatibilities at purchase time.

abuse.title: Abuse of the DMCA process
abuse.body: Knowingly false notices are punishable under § 512(f). RuleSell may publish redacted information about abusive notices in our annual transparency report (DSA Art. 15).

dmca.citations: 17 U.S.C. § 512(b)-(g) (DMCA); § 512(c)(2) (designated agent registration with the US Copyright Office); SPDX License List.

creatorAgreement.title: Creator Agreement
creatorAgreement.intro: This Creator Agreement applies in addition to our Terms of Service when you publish items on RuleSell. It covers fees, payouts, refunds, the licence you grant us, and what happens on termination.

eligibility.title: Becoming a Builder or Seller
eligibility.body: Builders publish free items. To become a Builder you must hold a verified account and accept this Agreement. Sellers also publish paid items and receive payouts. To become a Seller you must (a) accumulate at least 50 verified installs on a published free item, (b) complete trader KYC under DSA Art. 30 through Stripe Connect Express, and (c) verify your tax residence.

fees.title: Fees and revenue split
fees.body: RuleSell takes a 15% platform fee on every paid sale. For the first six months after a Seller's first paid sale, the platform fee is reduced to 10% as an early-creator incentive. Payment processing fees are absorbed by RuleSell. Reviewer cuts (2-3%) for Certified Devs, where applicable, are paid out of the platform fee, not the creator share.

payouts.title: Payouts
payouts.body: Earnings clear to your Stripe Connect balance after a 30-day hold to cover refunds and chargebacks. The minimum payout amount is the equivalent of $50 in your settlement currency. Payouts are processed monthly. Failed payouts roll forward to the next cycle.

refunds.title: Refunds
refunds.body: Buyers may request a refund within 14 days of purchase if they have not actively used the item. Refunds are processed against your earnings balance. Chargebacks issued by a buyer's card scheme are also debited from your balance.

license.title: License you grant to RuleSell
license.body: You retain ownership of your items. By publishing on RuleSell you grant us a worldwide, non-exclusive, royalty-free, sublicensable licence to host, distribute, display, perform, and transmit the items for the purpose of operating the marketplace and fulfilling buyer entitlements. The licence terminates with the item (subject to entitlements already granted to buyers).

warranties.title: Your warranties
warranties.body: You warrant that you own or have permission to publish each item, that the item does not infringe third-party rights, that it is safe to install, that the declared SPDX licence is accurate, and that you will indemnify RuleSell against claims arising out of a breach of these warranties.

trader.title: Trader status
trader.body: By selling paid items you confirm you are acting in your business or professional capacity. You will provide the trader information required by DSA Art. 30 through Stripe Connect Express, including legal name, registered address, contact details, registration number, and bank account. We may suspend paid sales if this information is missing or inaccurate.

termination.title: Termination
termination.body: Either party may terminate this Agreement at any time. On termination your items are unpublished. Pending payouts continue to be processed for 90 days to allow for refunds and chargebacks; afterwards the residual balance is paid out subject to the minimum threshold.

creatorAgreement.citations: DSA Art. 30-31 (Trader KYC, Regulation 2022/2065); GDPR Art. 6(1)(b) (contract performance basis); EU Consumer Rights Directive 2011/83/EU (14-day withdrawal period).

accessibility.title: Accessibility Statement
accessibility.intro: RuleSell is committed to being usable by everyone, regardless of disability or assistive technology. Our target is conformance with the Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG) 2.2 at Level AA, and with the European harmonised standard EN 301 549 where applicable.

target.title: Conformance target
target.body: WCAG 2.2 Level AA across all public pages, the dashboard, the publishing wizard, and all dialogs. Our colour contrast tokens meet the 4.5:1 ratio for body text and 3:1 for non-text UI. Every interactive element has a visible focus state, an accessible name, and works without a mouse.

testing.title: How we test
testing.body: We run automated accessibility audits with Lighthouse and axe-core in CI. We carry out manual keyboard sweeps before every release. Every dialog and form is verified with screen readers (NVDA on Windows, VoiceOver on macOS). New pages must meet the same gates before merging.

issues.title: Known issues
issues.body: We are honest about gaps. Current items: (1) the Quality bar animation does not yet announce intermediate values to screen readers; we play the final value only. (2) Some long-form legal pages do not yet have a localised reading-time indicator. (3) The publishing wizard's variant editor is keyboard navigable but the drag-to-reorder affordance is mouse-only; a keyboard equivalent is in progress.

report.title: How to report a barrier
report.body: If something on RuleSell is hard or impossible to use with your assistive technology, please email accessibility@rulesell.example or use the Report button on the affected page. We aim to acknowledge within 5 business days and to resolve substantive issues within 30 days. Where a fix takes longer we offer an alternative way of accessing the same information.

alternatives.title: Alternative formats
alternatives.body: Plain-text versions of every legal page are available on request. The marketplace catalog is available at /llms.txt and /llms-full.txt for agent-readable consumption.

exemption.title: Microenterprise exemption
exemption.body: RuleSell currently meets the European Accessibility Act microenterprise threshold (Directive 2019/882, Art. 4(5)). The exemption applies to certain service-provision obligations but not to the underlying accessibility expectations. We commit to WCAG 2.2 AA voluntarily and will update this section if our headcount or turnover crosses the threshold.

accessibility.citations: WCAG 2.2 Level AA (W3C Recommendation, 5 October 2023); EN 301 549 v3.2.1; Directive (EU) 2019/882 (European Accessibility Act); Art. 21 of the Charter of Fundamental Rights of the EU.

transparency.title: Transparency Report
transparency.intro: RuleSell publishes operational transparency information in line with the Digital Services Act. This page is updated monthly. Annual narrative reports are linked at the bottom.
transparency.mauNote: Rolling 6-month average of unique visitors located in the European Union, calculated from session-level analytics. Below the 45-million threshold for VLOP designation, but published anyway under DSA Art. 24(2).
transparency.ordersNote: We received zero binding orders to remove content or disclose user information in the reporting period.
transparency.annualLink: Download the 2025 annual transparency report (PDF stub)
transparency.methodologyBody: Counts are deduplicated by content ID. "Notices received" includes both Trusted Flagger reports and ordinary user reports. "Appeals" count cases that completed the full Statement-of-Reasons → in-product appeal cycle.

toolPicker.label: What do you use?
toolPicker.hint: Pick your tools — we'll show you what works

hero.title: Ship better AI, faster.
hero.subtitle: Quality-scored rules, MCPs, and tools — verified, installable in one command.

shelves.newThisWeekDesc: Fresh drops in the last 14 days
shelves.collectionsDesc: Themed bundles for the way you build

howItWorks.title: How it works
howItWorks.step1Desc: Find rules, MCPs, skills, and more for your environment.
howItWorks.step2Desc: Every asset is measured on token efficiency, security, and reliability.
howItWorks.step3Desc: Copy the install command. Works with Cursor, Windsurf, Claude, and more.

qualityShowcase.title: Quality is measured, not voted.
qualityShowcase.subtitle: Five automated signals replace star ratings and download counts.
qualityShowcase.tokenEfficiencyDesc: Measures actual token consumption, not self-reported estimates.
qualityShowcase.schemaCleanlinessDesc: JSON and YAML validation against published standards.
qualityShowcase.freshnessDesc: Penalizes stale assets. Rewards active maintenance.

creatorEconomics.title: Creators keep 85%.
creatorEconomics.body: No other AI asset marketplace pays creators. We take 15% — that's it. No listing fees, no featured-placement taxes. Payouts via Stripe Connect, monthly.

trustStrip.title: Built on verified trust
trustStrip.virusTotalDesc: Every upload scanned against 70+ antivirus engines.
trustStrip.semgrepDesc: Static analysis catches security anti-patterns before publishing.
trustStrip.verifiedReviewsDesc: Only users who installed an asset via CLI can leave reviews.
trustStrip.learnMore: Learn more about our trust model

finalCta.title: Ready to upgrade your workflow?
finalCta.subtitle: Browse quality-scored assets built by developers, for developers.

errors.title: We hit a snag loading the marketplace.

collections.heroSubtitle: Themed bundles handpicked by the RuleSell team and trusted creators — the best starting points for your workflow.

reviews.empty: No verified reviews yet. Only Certified Devs can write reviews after installing.

form.title: Write a verified review
form.headline: Headline
form.ineligible: Reviews are restricted to verified installers and Certified Devs. Learn more about certification.

notFound.title: We couldn't find that ruleset.
notFound.description: It may have been unpublished, renamed, or never existed.

report.label: Report
report.title: Report content
report.subtitle: Help us keep RuleSell safe and lawful. Reports go to our moderation team and are acted on without undue delay (DSA Art. 16).
report.descriptionPlaceholder: Tell us what you saw and where. The more specific you are, the faster we can act.
report.emailHelp: We use it only to follow up on your report.
report.declarationLabel: I declare that the information in this report is accurate and that I am acting in good faith. I understand that knowingly false reports may be acted upon under DSA Art. 23.
report.successBody: Reference {ref}. Our moderation team will review without undue delay (DSA Art. 16).
report.errorBody: Please try again. If the problem persists, email moderation@rulesell.example.

licenseWarning.nonCommercialPaid: Non-commercial licenses cannot be sold paid. Choose a commercial-compatible license to enable paid distribution.
licenseWarning.gplPurchase: GPL license — derivative works must remain open source under the same license.
licenseWarning.agplPurchase: AGPL license — network use triggers source-disclosure obligations for hosted derivatives.
licenseWarning.cc0Public: Public domain (CC0) — no restrictions on use, modification, or redistribution.

dsrExport.title: Export your personal data
dsrExport.subtitle: We'll bundle the data we hold on you into a single JSON file you can download. Required by GDPR Art. 20 (data portability) and CCPA § 1798.100 (right to know).
dsrExport.audit: We logged this request to our DSR audit trail.

dsrDelete.title: Delete your account
dsrDelete.consequences: Your published rulesets will be marked REMOVED. Reviews you wrote will be kept but anonymised. Pending payouts will be frozen and processed in line with the Creator Agreement. The deletion takes effect after a 30-day cooling-off period (GDPR Art. 17).
dsrDelete.scheduledBody: Your account will be deleted on {date}. You can cancel any time before then by signing in and contacting privacy@rulesell.example.

dsrCorrection.title: Correct your personal data
dsrCorrection.subtitle: Update fields we hold about you. Required by GDPR Art. 16 (rectification) and CCPA § 1798.106 (right to correct).

report.metaDescription: Submit a notice under DSA Article 16 for content hosted on RuleSell.
report.intro: Use this form to notify us of content you believe is illegal, infringes intellectual property, is unsafe, or otherwise violates our Acceptable Use Policy. We confirm receipt and act without undue delay (DSA Art. 16).
report.anchorAfterBody: (1) We confirm receipt with a reference number. (2) Our moderation team reviews against our Acceptable Use Policy and applicable law. (3) If we act on the content, the affected user receives a Statement of Reasons under DSA Art. 17. (4) Both parties may appeal through the in-product appeal flow.

devUsers.title: Persona Switcher
devUsers.subtitle: Development-only. Switch between role fixtures to verify the gating used by other builders. Persists in localStorage until reset.

visitor.label: Visitor
visitor.description: No auth. Browse only. Sees the marketing-style landing and the funnel strip prompt.

user.label: User (Free)
user.description: Verified 18+ account on the free tier. Can save, follow, vote, comment, purchase. No publishing rights.

pro.label: Pro
pro.description: User with active Pro subscription. Sees the Pro ring, advanced filters, private collections, install history.

builder.label: Builder (no paid yet)
builder.description: Builder with verified email. Can publish FREE items only — paywall to unlock paid sales is dormant.

builderEligible.label: Builder (eligible to upgrade)
builderEligible.description: Builder with 50+ verified installs across free items. Sees the "Upgrade to Seller" CTA and trader KYC entry point.

seller.label: Seller (Trader)
seller.description: Trader KYC complete + Stripe Connect verified. Can publish PAID items and receives payouts.

certified.label: Certified Dev
certified.description: 200+ reputation reviewer. Can write verified-install reviews, earns 2-3% reviewer cut on badged items.

teamOwner.label: Team Owner
teamOwner.description: Owns a multi-seat team. Sees team dashboard, shared earnings split, member management.

maintainer.label: Maintainer
maintainer.description: Verified GitHub OAuth + repo ownership. Can claim listings tied to verified OSS repos.

admin.label: Admin
admin.description: Platform staff. Sees moderation queue, transparency counters, and override controls.

checks.title: Verify gating
checks.subtitle: After switching, click these checks to confirm the persona shows the right gated UI elsewhere.

hero.title: The marketplace AI developers deserve.
hero.subtitle: Quality measured. Creators paid. Trust verified.

problem.title: The problem
problem.point1: AI tool directories list thousands of assets with no quality signals.
problem.point2: Creators earn nothing on every major platform.
problem.point3: Star ratings are gamed. Download counts are meaningless.
problem.point4: Install instructions are copy-paste nightmares across different tools.

solution.title: Our approach
solution.point1: Every asset measured on five automated quality signals.
solution.point2: Creators keep 85% of every paid sale.
solution.point3: Reviews restricted to verified installers only.
solution.point4: One-command CLI install across Cursor, Windsurf, Claude, and more.

features.qualityDesc: Token efficiency, install success, schema cleanliness, freshness, and security scan combined into a single score.
features.creatorsDesc: 85/15 revenue split with monthly Stripe payouts. No listing fees. No featured-placement taxes.
features.reviewsDesc: Only users who installed via CLI can rate. Written reviews require Certified Dev status.
features.crossEnvDesc: One listing works across Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf, Cline, Zed, and more.

business.title: Business model
business.freeDesc: Free forever. Publish and install free assets with no strings attached.
business.commissionDesc: We take 15% on paid sales. Creators keep the rest. No hidden fees.
business.proDesc: Private collections, full install history, and advanced filters. Not required to buy or sell.

team.title: The team
team.body: Built by Velkina Studio. We use our own platform daily.

hero.title: Trust is engineered, not assumed.
hero.subtitle: Every signal on the marketplace is earned through automated measurement, not self-reported or crowd-voted.

qualityScore.title: Quality Score
qualityScore.desc: A single number (0-100) summarizing five automated signals. It is the default sort on the marketplace — not stars, not downloads.
qualityScore.disclaimer: Quality scores are currently estimated from automated signals. We are working to improve accuracy.

pipeline.title: Security pipeline
pipeline.paidNote: Paid items go through an additional manual review before approval.

badges.title: Badges and marks
badges.itemBadgesDesc: Badges appear on cards and detail pages. They stack — an item can carry several at once.
badges.creatorMarksDesc: Marks appear on creator avatars and profile pages. They signal who someone is and how they earned their status.

reputation.title: Reputation levels
reputation.desc: Reputation is earned by contributing to the marketplace. Every verified install, review, and community action adds points.

reviews.title: Review integrity
reviews.desc: You cannot review something you have not used. Written reviews are further restricted to Certified Devs.

hero.title: Earn by recommending what you actually use.
hero.subtitle: Share your referral link. If someone buys through it within 30 days, you earn a commission.

howItWorks.title: How it works
howItWorks.step1: Sign up for the affiliate program

personas.title: Who it's for
personas.contentDesc: YouTube reviewers, bloggers, and tutorial authors who recommend dev tools.
personas.newsletterDesc: Tech newsletter writers with audiences who trust their tool recommendations.
personas.communityDesc: Discord and Slack community managers who curate resources for members.

affiliates.selfReferral: Self-referral is allowed — if you promote your own items, you earn both creator revenue and the affiliate bonus.

hero.title: Install AI assets from your terminal.
hero.subtitle: The CLI detects your environment and writes config to the right file. No copy-paste, no manual setup.

gettingStarted.title: Getting started
gettingStarted.step1Desc: Requires Node.js 18+. Open source at github.com/rulesell/cli.
gettingStarted.step2Desc: Run rulesell auth to connect your account. Free assets work without auth.
gettingStarted.step3Desc: Pick any asset from the marketplace and run the install command.

features.freeDesc: Install any free asset without authentication. One command, done.
features.paidDesc: CLI opens your browser for OAuth and payment. Token downloads automatically.
features.envDesc: Detects Cursor, Windsurf, Claude Code, VS Code, and more. Override with --env.
features.commandsDesc: Search, list, update, and remove. Everything from your terminal.

cli.note: The CLI package lives at packages/rulesell-cli/ in the monorepo. All commands work against mock data in development. Production release planned for v2.
